articles.title: World's tallest timber tower tops out in Sydney
articles.summary: The Atlassian Central skyscraper, designed by SHoP Architects and BVN Architecture, in Sydney, Australia, has become the world's tallest hybrid timber tower. Designed as the Sydney headquarters for technology company Atlassian, the 180-metre-high office tower topped out earlier this week. articles.title: Rike Park Music Theater in Tbilisi and CRA's Residential Tower in Seoul: This Week's Review
articles.summary: This week in architecture , discussions around the lifespan of buildings, the evolution of cultural institutions, and climate-responsive design highlighted how contemporary practice increasingly extends beyond the moment of construction. From debates over demolition and adaptive reuse to new museums that integrate landscape and environmental performance, the week's developments reflect shifting priorities around permanence, transformation, and public value. Alongside these projects, major institutional announcements, from the RIBA Stirling Prize shortlist to the election of a new UIA leadershi

articles.title: Green Lodge  / Stylus Architects
articles.summary: Green Lodge began with a single instruction from a long-standing contractor client: "Do whatever you want, but it has to be quick." The result is a house on the edge of Putney Heath in South West London that is drawn into the ground rather than asserting itself above it. Faced with a sensitive and tightly constrained site, Stylus Architects took the building below ground, maintaining a modest external presence whilst unlocking a generous family home beneath the surface.

articles.title: Dallas Evaluates Repair and Demolition Options for I.M. Pei’s Modernist City Hall
articles.summary: Since August 2025, debate has intensified in Dallas , United States , over the future of one of its modern landmarks : I.M. Pei & Partners' Dallas City Hall . As of today, the Dallas City Council will continue weighing whether to repair, sell, or demolish the 47-year-old building, following growing concerns over long-deferred maintenance and the need for major investment. In late October 2025, council members began public listening sessions and committee meetings to gather resident input. Preservationists and some council members urged a full study of repair options and historic landmarking, w

articles.title: South of Everything: Architecture and Territorial Presence in Antarctica
articles.summary: Antarctica has no government, no permanent population, and no economy. It also, since 1959, has had no legally recognized owner as the Antarctic Treaty freezes every territorial claim on the continent. On paper, this makes Antarctica the closest thing on Earth to a true commons. In practice, close to thirty countries currently operate more than seventy research stations there, several of them within a short flight of one another, and one of those stations has a school, a chapel, a cemetery, and a civil registry office that has recorded eleven births since 1978.

articles.title: Apartment in Uehara / ULTRA STUDIO
articles.summary: Tokyo's streetscape is often criticized—or praised—for its lack of uniformity, as arbitrary buildings crowd each individual plot. This chaotic quality is frequently cited as the city's defining feature. Yet when viewed as a whole, it reveals a strangely homogeneous, generic landscape devoid of clear intent—an endless expanse of white, gray, beige, and brown structures. For instance, though its origins remain obscure, concrete clad in beige tile has become the standard facade for Tokyo apartment buildings. It is unclear when this scenery first emerged, but for us, it has simply become a "natura

articles.title: JA House / Ayoub Architects
articles.summary: JA House is shaped by the unique character of its site in southern Amman , overlooking one of the city's largest public parks with uninterrupted panoramic views. The steep topography and the preserved oak trees along the site's perimeter inspired a concept centered on a green terraced platform that extends the landscape into the architecture while naturally embedding the main living spaces within the terrain.

articles.title: The Responsibility of Optimism
articles.summary: Recorded live at Milan Design Week 2026 in cooperation with INDX|GLOBAL , the ninth episode of the Room For Dreams podcast brings together architects Rajiv Parekh of Red Architects, Hiren Patel of HPAD, and Rudraksh Charan of 42mm Architecture to discuss how design can serve as a powerful tool for emotional resilience and collective healing.

articles.title: Deadwood Hut / Insight Architects
articles.summary: Shelter for All Beings — Deadwood Hut is an installation exhibited in the Nankunshan-Luofushan Rim Pioneer Zone Architectural Art Project, Shelter for All Beings, and is located along the Xutian River in Huizhou . The project re-examines the term "shelter" and seeks to move beyond the inherent anthropocentrism of the primitive hut, creating an artificial structure that accommodates other‑than‑human beings. Centered on deadwood, the installation brings overlooked abandoned timber along the riverside back into view and transforms it into an open habitat for insects, plants, microorganisms, and o

articles.title: Veneciana Escalada Drive-Thru Ice Cream Shop / DRM Arquitectura
articles.summary: The project involved the expansion of an existing commercial building to create a drive-through ice cream shop, a typology in which both pedestrians and vehicles enter, circulate, and remain within the site.

articles.title: Light of Amazonia Chapel / Rogoski Arquitetura
articles.summary: The Amazon Light Chapel was born from the client's desire to create a space that would represent a "point of light in the Amazon." Situated on the shores of a lake, amidst a remnant of native forest in the region of Cacoal , Rondônia, the architecture establishes a relationship of contemplation and profound respect with the landscape.

articles.title: Casa Para Ver La Peña / Tenter Studio
articles.summary: From the street, the house appears restrained and silent. The stone volumes are barely pronounced, avoiding any emphatic gesture. The architecture does not seek to announce itself; it holds back. It is only upon crossing the property line that the project begins to reveal itself.

articles.title: Leblon Apartment / Pascali Semerdjian Arquitetos
articles.summary: Originally inhabited by a family, the Leblon apartment underwent a complete transformation to meet the needs of its new owner. The main objective of the project was to integrate and expand the social areas, including the master suite, which can now be connected to the living room via bifold doors.

articles.title: Espace Ambroise Croizat / TAUTEM Architecture
articles.summary: Between the Arceaux and Figuerolles districts, TAUTEM transforms the Center for Social Experimentation and Innovation (CEIS) into an architecture of care and dignity, attentive to the Mediterranean climate and to the memory of the site.

articles.title: DeFF's Diminutive Premium Pen
articles.summary: As with Studio Neat's minimalist Mark Two Pen , Japanese design firm DeFF has also designed a tiny but premium writing instrument. Their Refill Pen Jacket Extra Short (the name loses a little something in translation) goes from 75mm (2.95") closed to 125mm (4.92") in the writing/drawing configuration. The body of the the diminutive pen is stainless steel, and it's offered with multiple finishes: Brass, grey titanium, and anodized aluminum in black, aquamarine or ruby. It takes standard D1 and 4C refills. They also offer engraving. There's even a little companion refill case. The pen runs $50 r

articles.title: A Security Camera Designed to Indicate When It's Not Watching (You)
articles.summary: Home security cameras are intended to record intruders, not the homeowners themselves. It's creepy to think that someone could hack into them to observe you, but that's the trade-off these products require. One departure is this Smart Camera 4 Zoom by Xiaomi. It has two lenses that resemble eyes; one is an 8 MP ultra-wide and the other a 5 MP telephoto, so it can cover an entire room but still zoom in on people. The motion-tracking camera can pan and tilt. Most notably, the camera has been designed so that homeowners know when it's not watching them. When privacy is desired, homeowners press a

articles.title: AIM's Deconstructed Kitchen Faucet
articles.summary: For thirty years, Wenzhou Dinggu Technology Co. made molds for manufacturers of bathroom fittings. In 2018, they decided to do their own thing and lean into design, forming a spin-off called AIM Faucet. The company now does OEM/ODM design and manufacturing for other brands. The strategy has paid off, and this year the company's won an iF Design Award for their deconstructed three-section kitchen faucet : The faucet features a tri-sectional rotating system, where the faucet body, connecting arm, and spout move independently. This allows 360° sink coverage, reaching every corner of the sink and

articles.title: 2026 Nightingale Awards Product Competition Accepting Entries - Healthcare Design Magazine
articles.summary: The 2026 Nightingale Awards are now accepting entries for their annual product competition, inviting designers worldwide to submit healthcare-related innovations. Participants are encouraged to showcase improvements in device usability, patient safety, or operational efficiency through their designs. The competition aims to highlight emerging talent and promote sustainable, user‑centered solutions in medicine.

articles.title: Kyobo's Book Scent Brand 'The Scent of Page' Wins Red Dot Design Award - Seoul Economic Daily
articles.summary: Kyobo Bookstore’s signature scent line, The Scent of Page, has earned a coveted Red Dot Design Award for its inventive approach to book fragrance. The Skyblue packaging and delivery concept successfully merge olfactory experience with literary nostalgia. This accolade positions the brand as a frontrunner in sensory branding within the publishing industry.
